meeting.description: Updates on upcoming events and student welfare.

meeting.title: Term 1 Retrospective
meeting.description: Discussing what went well and areas for improvement.

meeting.title: Weekly Staff Meeting
meeting.description: Regular weekly sync for all staff.

observation.notes: Well-structured lesson with clear objectives. Excellent use of visuals.
observation.actionStep: Incorporate more open-ended questions to deepen student inquiry.

observation.notes: Positive classroom environment. Students felt comfortable sharing ideas.
observation.actionStep: Work on transition times between activities to improve pacing.

observation.notes: Outstanding microplan aligned with curriculum goals. Clear progression of concepts.
observation.actionStep: Add more real-world application problems to strengthen conceptual understanding.

observation.notes: Good management of student behaviour. Warm-up activity was effective.
observation.actionStep: Explore brain break strategies to maintain focus during long problem sets.

observation.notes: Creative lesson design with strong text selection. Students were engaged readers.
observation.actionStep: Introduce more structured debate and argument-building exercises.

observation.notes: Strong culture for learning. Students were proud to share creative writing.
observation.actionStep: Implement peer editing workshops to strengthen collaborative literacy.

goal.title: Improve Inquiry-Based Learning
goal.description: Implement at least 3 inquiry-based lesson cycles per term using the 5E model.
goal.actionStep: Complete online module on inquiry-based learning and trial one lesson per week.

goal.title: Formative Assessment Mastery
goal.description: Use exit tickets and peer feedback consistently across all Science units.
goal.actionStep: Design a bank of 20 exit tickets aligned to curriculum standards.

goal.title: Real-World Maths Integration
goal.description: Connect all Maths units to real-world scenarios through project-based tasks.
goal.actionStep: Design one real-world project per unit using financial literacy themes.

goal.title: Differentiated Instruction
goal.description: Implement tiered tasks to support advanced and struggling learners simultaneously.
goal.actionStep: Attend the Differentiated Instruction Masterclass in March 2026.

goal.title: Student Voice Through Writing
goal.description: Build a student portfolio programme where learners publish one piece each term.
goal.actionStep: Set up a classroom blog and establish a weekly writing workshop routine.

goal.title: Reading Comprehension Strategies
goal.description: Explicitly teach 5 comprehension strategies and track student growth each term.
goal.actionStep: Implement a reading log system and weekly comprehension strategy lesson.

assessmentQuestion.prompt: What is the purpose of a Master Plan?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["To map long-term content standards","To share the collection of learning experiences in a Unit","To plan out the hour by hour instruction in the classroom","To design assessments for the Unit"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: To share the collection of learning experiences in a Unit

assessmentQuestion.prompt: How does a Microplan support lesson delivery?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["It gives scripted lesson scripts for teachers to follow","It offers day-wise instructional guidance","It shows daily assessments and worksheets in a lesson","It tracks student progress over the unit"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: It shows daily assessments and worksheets in a lesson

assessmentQuestion.prompt: What is the primary purpose of assessments at Ekya?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["To rank students","To support learning and growth","To assign punishments","To complete paperwork"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: To support learning and growth

assessmentQuestion.prompt: Which of the following would you categorize as an Authentic Task?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Solving 10 algebra equations on a worksheet","Memorizing definitions of literary devices","Writing a persuasive letter to the local government about a community issue","Matching vocabulary words with their meanings in a quiz"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: Writing a persuasive letter to the local government about a community issue

assessmentQuestion.prompt: How should teachers identify which instructional tool/strategy to use?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["By choosing the one that is most popular among students","By selecting a tool that aligns with the learning objective and student needs","By using the same tool for all lessons to ensure consistency","By picking the tool that requires the least preparation time"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: By selecting a tool that aligns with the learning objective and student needs

assessmentQuestion.prompt: Which of the following scenarios effectively describe a flipped classroom?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["The teacher delivers a lecture on Newton’s Laws during class, and students are assigned textbook questions to complete as homework.","Students read an article and complete a short quiz on supply and demand at home. In class, they participate in a market simulation to apply the concepts and reflect on pricing strategies."]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: Students read an article and complete a short quiz on supply and demand at home. In class, they participate in a market simulation to apply the concepts and reflect on pricing strategies.

assessmentQuestion.prompt: Why should a teacher differentiate instruction? (Select all that apply)
assessmentQuestion.options: ["To address the diverse learning needs, interests, and readiness levels of students","To ensure all students have equal opportunities to access and engage with the content","To make lesson planning easier and faster for the teacher","To support students in progressing at their own pace and achieving mastery"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: ["To address the diverse learning needs, interests, and readiness levels of students","To ensure all students have equal opportunities to access and engage with the content","To support students in progressing at their own pace and achieving mastery"]

assessmentQuestion.prompt: Which of the following scenarios showcase an effective use of student data to make a classroom decision?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["A teacher gives the same homework to all students, regardless of their quiz performance.","After reviewing assessment results, a teacher forms small groups for reteaching specific concepts.","A teacher designs a lesson based only on the textbook sequence, without reviewing past student performance.","A teacher notices some students are struggling but decides to wait until the end-of-term exam to take action."]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: After reviewing assessment results, a teacher forms small groups for reteaching specific concepts.

assessmentQuestion.prompt: Which of the following would be considered data-informed instructional next steps for a quiz with mixed results? (Select all that apply)
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Pair high-scoring students with low-scoring students for peer tutoring next lesson","Identify question types that the lower-scoring students struggled with and reteach using a different strategy","Review the quiz with the entire class, focusing only on the questions most students got wrong","Create targeted small-group sessions for low-scoring students while tracking participation in the next lesson","Assume the low scores are due to poor effort and plan to move forward with the curriculum"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: ["Pair high-scoring students with low-scoring students for peer tutoring next lesson","Identify question types that the lower-scoring students struggled with and reteach using a different strategy","Create targeted small-group sessions for low-scoring students while tracking participation in the next lesson"]

assessmentQuestion.prompt: Which of the following would be considered qualitative or observational data that a teacher can use to inform instruction? (Select all that apply)
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Quiz scores","Exit ticket responses","Anecdotal notes about student behaviour during group work","A student’s attendance record","Peer feedback from a class activity"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: ["Exit ticket responses","Anecdotal notes about student behaviour during group work","Peer feedback from a class activity"]

assessmentQuestion.prompt: Why should teachers use graphic organizers in the classroom?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["To decorate classroom displays","To help students memorize large chunks of information","To support students in organizing and visualizing their thinking","To reduce the amount of content teachers need to cover"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: To support students in organizing and visualizing their thinking

assessmentQuestion.prompt: Which of the following most accurately aligns with the principle of "assessment as learning"?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Evaluating students through a summative test at the end of a unit","Using a rubric to assign a final grade for a project","Having students reflect on their progress and set goals during the task","Giving feedback only after the entire class has completed the assignment"]

assessmentQuestion.prompt: A teacher wants to improve the reliability and transparency of scoring student projects. Which combination of actions would best support this goal?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Provide oral feedback only and avoid numerical scores","Design a rubric with clear criteria and performance levels, and share it with students before the assessment","Allow students to choose their own grading criteria based on project type","Use peer assessment without teacher moderation"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: Design a rubric with clear criteria and performance levels, and share it with students before the assessment

assessmentQuestion.prompt: In providing feedback on an authentic task, which of the following best reflects the growth mindset approach advocated in the session?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["\"You scored 6/10 because you missed key details.\"","\"Great work! You're naturally talented in this subject.\"","\"You're not quite there yet—try expanding your analysis with examples like the one we discussed in class.\"","\"This wasn't good enough. You need to focus more next time.\""]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: "You're not quite there yet—try expanding your analysis with examples like the one we discussed in class."

assessmentQuestion.options: ["T-Chart","KWL Chart","I Used to Think / Now I Know","Exit Ticket"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: I Used to Think / Now I Know

assessmentQuestion.prompt: You are about to begin a Grade 6 math lesson on calculating the area of a triangle. Which of the following is the most effective way to activate relevant prior knowledge for this lesson?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Ask students to recall the types of angles and triangles they learned in art class","Begin with a real-life problem involving floor tiling and provide the area formula directly","Review how to calculate the area of a rectangle and relate it to how a triangle is half of that","Ask students to list all geometry terms they remember from the previous year"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: Review how to calculate the area of a rectangle and relate it to how a triangle is half of that

assessmentQuestion.prompt: Which of the following practices would violate the assessment protocol in Schoology, as per institutional guidelines?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Adding an ungraded worksheet to a unit folder","Publishing a pre-created E2 assessment in the assessment folder","Creating a new assessment category called 'Homework' in the assessment folder","Sharing instructions for assessments in the description field"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: Creating a new assessment category called 'Homework' in the assessment folder

assessmentQuestion.prompt: When should an educator use a 'Page' instead of an 'Assignment' in Schoology?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["To assign a graded task with a due date","To post session highlights or share additional resources","To collect a writing submission from students","To assess students on a quiz from the learning plan"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: To post session highlights or share additional resources

assessmentQuestion.prompt: A teacher wants students to critically engage with a topic and respond to their peers' perspectives. Which Schoology feature should they use, and why?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Quiz, because it allows individual timed responses","Assignment, because it supports group submissions","Discussion, because it facilitates visible peer interaction and threaded replies","Page, because it lets students edit shared content collaboratively"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: Discussion, because it facilitates visible peer interaction and threaded replies

assessmentQuestion.prompt: Which of the following best describes the Flipped Classroom approach?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Students watch a lecture in class and complete activities as homework.","Students create presentations and teach their peers in class.","Students are introduced to new content at home and engage in application during class.","Teachers deliver live lectures online, and students take notes at home."]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: Students are introduced to new content at home and engage in application during class.

assessmentQuestion.prompt: A teacher flips a Grade 10 History lesson by assigning students a timeline and video on the causes of World War II to review before class. Which of the following in-class activities would best support higher-order thinking aligned with the flipped approach?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Students answer multiple-choice questions based on the timeline","Students work in teams to rank and justify the most significant causes of WWII","The teacher summarizes key events while students take structured notes","Students copy key dates and events into their notebooks for future reference"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: Students work in teams to rank and justify the most significant causes of WWII

assessmentQuestion.prompt: If a few students enter class without completing the asynchronous pre-task, which of the following is the best response from the teacher?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Have them quietly observe the group work without participating","Briefly pair them with a peer who completed the task for a quick catch-up","Move ahead without them — they'll learn next time","Cancel the in-class task and switch to a teacher-led lecture"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: Briefly pair them with a peer who completed the task for a quick catch-up

assessmentQuestion.prompt: Which of the following lessons would be least suitable for a flipped classroom approach?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Introducing a new math topic like solving quadratic equations for the first time","Reviewing a previously taught topic through group problem-solving","Having students conduct a science investigation based on prior video prep","Debating a social issue after students have read multiple perspectives"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: Introducing a new math topic like solving quadratic equations for the first time

assessmentQuestion.prompt: A teacher uses a flipped classroom model for a Grade 11 Science lesson. Students watch a video explaining the structure of DNA before class. In class, they work in pairs to model the DNA strand using coloured beads and then explain the function of each part to their peers. Which of the following best explains how this lesson reflects the principles of flipped learning aligned with Bloom's Taxonomy and the Gradual Release of Responsibility?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["It allows students to independently recall information during class with no teacher support.","It shifts content delivery to home so that in class, students can engage in hands-on and peer-based application.","It replaces teacher instruction entirely with peer activities to promote independence.","It provides a teacher-led recap followed by note-taking to reinforce memory."]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: It shifts content delivery to home so that in class, students can engage in hands-on and peer-based application.

assessmentQuestion.prompt: You start the class by asking students to share anything positive that has happened in the day or the past week. Which culture practice is this?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Affirmations","Good Things","Gratitude Sharing","One minute mindfulness"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: Good Things

assessmentQuestion.prompt: What is the primary purpose of a classroom social contract?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["To enforce discipline through fixed teacher-created rules","To outline school-mandated behavior guidelines","To co-create shared expectations that promote respect and responsibility","To reward students for academic performance only"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: To co-create shared expectations that promote respect and responsibility

assessmentQuestion.prompt: Which of the following best explains the purpose of explicitly teaching routines during the first 15 days of school?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["To keep students occupied while academic planning is finalized","To ensure students can work independently and transitions run smoothly","To reduce the need for classroom norms","To prepare students for assessments early in the term"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: To ensure students can work independently and transitions run smoothly

assessmentQuestion.prompt: On Day 3 of school, a teacher notices students are still interrupting during discussions. Which action is most aligned with the Classroom Norms essential?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Giving students a warning and moving on","Sending a note home to parents about expected behavior","Asking students to stay silent for the rest of the activity","Re-teaching the classroom's agreed-upon discussion norms using role-play"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: Re-teaching the classroom's agreed-upon discussion norms using role-play

assessmentQuestion.prompt: A teacher begins each day with a "Mood Meter" and a short mindfulness moment. Which two essentials from the First 15 Days framework does this most directly support?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Academic Engagement and Routines","SEL Integration and Relationships","Classroom Norms and Academic Engagement","Routines and Assessment"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: SEL Integration and Relationships

assessmentQuestion.prompt: Day 1 Snapshot of a classroom: Students are greeted at the door; name tags and a seating plan are provided; teacher introduces classroom rules and explains schedule; students complete an independent "All About Me" worksheet; the day ends with a classroom tour. Which of the following statements is the most appropriate evaluation of this Day 1 plan?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["It covers all 5 essentials effectively and does not need changes","It includes routines and academic engagement but lacks opportunities for SEL and co-construction of norms","It focuses on SEL and norms but does not introduce any academic activities","It builds strong academic engagement but ignores relationships and routines"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: It includes routines and academic engagement but lacks opportunities for SEL and co-construction of norms

assessmentQuestion.prompt: Which of the following best defines 'process differentiation'?

assessmentQuestion.prompt: What is the purpose of strategic student grouping in differentiated instruction?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["To group all high-performing students together for advanced tasks","To randomly assign groups for easier classroom management","To intentionally support student needs through targeted peer interactions","To avoid using different materials or instructions"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: To intentionally support student needs through targeted peer interactions

assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: The educator did not link the Learning Area (LA) and unit correctly while setting up the microplan

assessmentQuestion.prompt: An educator notices that their lesson plan lacks alignment with the unit's Enduring Understandings and Essential Questions. Based on the C&I platform structure, what is the most appropriate next step to realign the plan?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Modify the microplan using AI-generated content without referring to the unit plan","Review Stage 1 of the unit plan for key understandings and questions, and revise the microplan accordingly","Skip to Stage 3 and add more activities to the microplan","Request the academic coordinator to rewrite the microplan"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: Review Stage 1 of the unit plan for key understandings and questions, and revise the microplan accordingly

assessmentQuestion.prompt: Which of the following would you categorise as an Authentic Task?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Solving 10 algebra equations on a worksheet","Memorizing definitions of literary devices","Writing a persuasive letter to the local government about a community issue","Matching vocabulary words with their meanings in a quiz"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: Writing a persuasive letter to the local government about a community issue

assessmentQuestion.prompt: Which of the following most accurately aligns with the principle of "assessment as learning"?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Evaluating students through a summative test at the end of a unit","Using a rubric to assign a final grade for a project","Having students reflect on their progress and set goals during the task","Giving feedback only after the entire class has completed the assignment"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: Having students reflect on their progress and set goals during the task

assessmentQuestion.prompt: A teacher wants to improve the reliability and transparency of scoring student projects. Which combination of actions would best support this goal?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Provide oral feedback only and avoid numerical scores","Design a rubric with clear criteria and performance levels, and share it with students before the assessment","Allow students to choose their own grading criteria based on project type","Use peer assessment without teacher moderation"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: Design a rubric with clear criteria and performance levels, and share it with students before the assessment

assessmentQuestion.prompt: In providing feedback on an authentic task, which of the following best reflects the growth mindset approach advocated in the session?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["\"You scored 6/10 because you missed key details.\"","\"Great work! You're naturally talented in this subject.\"","\"You're not quite there yet—try expanding your analysis with examples like the one we discussed in class.\"","\"This wasn't good enough. You need to focus more next time.\""]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: "You're not quite there yet—try expanding your analysis with examples like the one we discussed in class."

assessmentQuestion.prompt: Ms. Iyer is teaching a Grade 7 Science lesson on the Water Cycle. She wants her students to understand the sequential stages (evaporation, condensation, precipitation, collection) and how each stage leads to the next. She also wants to help them visualise this process. Which type of graphic organiser would best support student understanding in this scenario?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Venn Diagram","Cause and Effect Chart","A Cycle Diagram","T-Chart"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: A Cycle Diagram

assessmentQuestion.prompt: Mr. Khan is teaching Character Development in a novel. He wants his students to compare two main characters in terms of their values, motivations, and growth through the story. Which graphic organiser would best help students analyse and compare the characters?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Concept Map","Flowchart","Timeline","Venn Diagram"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: Venn Diagram

assessmentQuestion.prompt: How should teachers identify which instructional tool/strategy to use?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["By choosing the one that is most popular among students","By selecting a tool that aligns with the learning objective and student needs","By using the same tool for all lessons to ensure consistency","By picking the tool that requires the least preparation time"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: By selecting a tool that aligns with the learning objective and student needs

assessmentQuestion.prompt: Before starting a new unit on Algebra, Mr. Sharma wants to quickly understand students' prior knowledge and identify misconceptions. Which strategy would best support his goal?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["See Think Wonder","Entry Ticket","Think-Pair-Share","Do Now"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: Entry Ticket

assessmentQuestion.prompt: During a lesson on climate change, Mr. Bose poses the question: "How do human activities contribute to global warming?" Which of the following strategy should he use?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Think-Pair-Share","KWL Chart","Entry Ticket","I Used to Think / Now I Know"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: Think-Pair-Share

assessmentQuestion.prompt: You have just finished a science lesson on renewable and non-renewable energy sources. You want students to reflect on their prior misconceptions and consolidate their new understanding. Which of the following strategies would be most appropriate for this purpose?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["T-Chart","KWL Chart","I Used to Think / Now I Know","Exit Ticket"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: I Used to Think / Now I Know

assessmentQuestion.prompt: You are about to begin a Grade 6 math lesson on calculating the area of a triangle. Which of the following is the most effective way to activate relevant prior knowledge for this lesson?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Ask students to recall the types of angles and triangles they learned in art class","Begin with a real-life problem involving floor tiling and provide the area formula directly","Review how to calculate the area of a rectangle and relate it to how a triangle is half of that","Ask students to list all geometry terms they remember from the previous year"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: Review how to calculate the area of a rectangle and relate it to how a triangle is half of that

assessmentQuestion.prompt: Which of the following practices would violate the assessment protocol in Schoology, as per institutional guidelines?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Adding an ungraded worksheet to a unit folder","Publishing a pre-created E2 assessment in the assessment folder","Creating a new assessment category called 'Homework' in the assessment folder","Sharing instructions for assessments in the description field"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: Creating a new assessment category called 'Homework' in the assessment folder

assessmentQuestion.prompt: When should an educator use a 'Page' instead of an 'Assignment' in Schoology?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["To assign a graded task with a due date","To post session highlights or share additional resources","To collect a writing submission from students","To assess students on a quiz from the learning plan"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: To post session highlights or share additional resources

assessmentQuestion.prompt: A teacher wants students to critically engage with a topic and respond to their peers' perspectives. Which Schoology feature should they use, and why?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Quiz, because it allows individual timed responses","Assignment, because it supports group submissions","Discussion, because it facilitates visible peer interaction and threaded replies","Page, because it lets students edit shared content collaboratively"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: Discussion, because it facilitates visible peer interaction and threaded replies

assessmentQuestion.prompt: You start the class by asking students to share anything positive that has happened in the day or the past week. Which culture practice is this?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Affirmations","Good Things","Gratitude Sharing","One minute mindfulness"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: Good Things

assessmentQuestion.prompt: What is the primary purpose of a classroom social contract?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["To enforce discipline through fixed teacher-created rules","To outline school-mandated behavior guidelines","To co-create shared expectations that promote respect and responsibility","To reward students for academic performance only"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: To co-create shared expectations that promote respect and responsibility

assessmentQuestion.prompt: Which of the following best explains the purpose of explicitly teaching routines during the first 15 days of school?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["To keep students occupied while academic planning is finalized","To ensure students can work independently and transitions run smoothly","To reduce the need for classroom norms","To prepare students for assessments early in the term"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: To ensure students can work independently and transitions run smoothly

assessmentQuestion.prompt: On Day 3 of school, a teacher notices students are still interrupting during discussions. Which action is most aligned with the Classroom Norms essential?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Giving students a warning and moving on","Sending a note home to parents about expected behavior","Asking students to stay silent for the rest of the activity","Re-teaching the classroom's agreed-upon discussion norms using role-play"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: Re-teaching the classroom's agreed-upon discussion norms using role-play

assessmentQuestion.prompt: A teacher begins each day with a "Mood Meter" and a short mindfulness moment. Which two essentials from the First 15 Days framework does this most directly support?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Academic Engagement and Routines","SEL Integration and Relationships","Classroom Norms and Academic Engagement","Routines and Assessment"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: SEL Integration and Relationships

assessmentQuestion.prompt: Day 1 Snapshot of a classroom: Students are greeted at the door; name tags and a seating plan are provided; teacher introduces classroom rules and explains schedule; students complete an independent "All About Me" worksheet; the day ends with a classroom tour. Which of the following statements is the most appropriate evaluation of this Day 1 plan?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["It covers all 5 essentials effectively and does not need changes","It includes routines and academic engagement but lacks opportunities for SEL and co-construction of norms","It focuses on SEL and norms but does not introduce any academic activities","It builds strong academic engagement but ignores relationships and routines"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: It includes routines and academic engagement but lacks opportunities for SEL and co-construction of norms

assessmentQuestion.prompt: During the first week of school, you notice that Meera, a quiet student, hasn't spoken up in class discussions. Using the 5x5 strategy, what would be the most appropriate first step?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Immediately call on Meera during the next discussion to encourage participation","Send a note to Meera's parents to ask about her home life","Spend time reflecting on Meera's behavior patterns and consider what she might be experiencing","Move her seat closer to the front of the class so you can monitor her better"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: Spend time reflecting on Meera's behavior patterns and consider what she might be experiencing

assessmentQuestion.prompt: Which of the following best defines 'process differentiation'?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Changing the physical space in which students learn","Allowing students to work on different topics","Giving students different ways to show their learning","Giving students different ways to make sense of the same content"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: Giving students different ways to make sense of the same content

assessmentQuestion.prompt: What is the purpose of strategic student grouping in differentiated instruction?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["To group all high-performing students together for advanced tasks","To randomly assign groups for easier classroom management","To intentionally support student needs through targeted peer interactions","To avoid using different materials or instructions"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: To intentionally support student needs through targeted peer interactions

assessmentQuestion.prompt: A teacher assigns all students the same story to read. Group A uses sentence starters to respond, Group B discusses the story in pairs, and Group C writes an essay. What type of differentiation is most clearly visible?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Content","Process","Product","Learning environment"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: Process

assessmentQuestion.prompt: In a classroom, Group 1 writes a few describing sentences with the teacher, Group 2 writes a paragraph using guiding questions, and Group 3 writes a descriptive letter independently. The same garden picture is used for all. What is the student characteristic being considered for Differentiation?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["Learning Profile","Readiness","Interest","All of the above"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: Readiness

assessmentQuestion.prompt: Which of the following is a typical step in the teacher observation process?
assessmentQuestion.options: ["School leader visits followed by anonymous feedback","Observation, feedback and follow up conversation","Group grading of lesson plans by peers","Mandatory training before each observation"]
assessmentQuestion.correctAnswer: Observation, feedback and follow up conversation

assessmentQuestion.prompt: What is the purpose of your learning area? Mention the learning area and its purpose in 3-4 sentences.
List any 1 Learning Area Tool of your choice and explain the purpose of the tool and how it is used.
